title: Query-level ranking by race lap instead of pure binary logloss
summary: At each lap, only a small subset of cars are realistic pit candidates.
rationale: At each lap, only a small subset of cars are realistic pit candidates. CatBoost supports `QueryAUC` and pairwise/groupwise ranking, so grouping by race-lap can optimize who should be ranked highest for an immediate stop rather than only global class likelihood.
implementation_hint: Create query groups like `(Race_Year, LapNumber)` or `(Race_Year, LapNumber, compound_regime)` and train a ranking model or a classifier monitored by `QueryAUC`; optionally blend its OOF scores with the existing probability model.
expected_effect: Should help in crowded decision points where many rows share similar static heuristics but only a few drivers are true next-lap pitters.
risk: If queries are too small or noisy, ranking may underperform plain classification; only keep it if OOF and public LB both improve.
